name: Pixiu
description: The Pixiu, also known as Pi Yao, is a mythical hybrid creature considered a powerful protector and benefactor in Chinese Feng Shui. It resembles a winged lion with a fierce countenance, often described as having the head of a dragon, the body of a lion, and the legs of a Qilin. Some depictions include wings and a tail. The Pixiu has a voracious appetite for gold, silver, and jewels, which is believed to symbolize its ability to attract wealth. A significant feature of the Pixiu is its lack of an anus, meaning it can only ingest but not excrete, further solidifying its association with accumulating and retaining wealth. It is considered a loyal and obedient creature, fiercely protective of its owner and warding off evil spirits. Pixiu statues and amulets are commonly displayed in homes and businesses to attract prosperity, good fortune, and protection from negative influences. The creature’s powerful and auspicious symbolism makes it a highly revered figure in Chinese culture, representing both wealth and safety. Different variations of the Pixiu exist, some with one horn (male) and others with two horns (female), each with slightly different interpretations of their protective and wealth-attracting powers. The Pixiu's association with the God of Wealth further enhances its significance in Chinese folklore and cultural practices.
subtitle: Wealth, Protection, and Good Fortune
appearance: A winged lion-like creature with the head of a dragon, the body of a lion, and the legs of a Qilin. It often has scales, a fierce expression, and a tail. Some depictions show one or two horns.
habitat: Typically found guarding homes, businesses, and sacred spaces. They exist primarily as statues and amulets, imbued with spiritual power.
behavior: Loyal, protective, and obedient. It is fiercely defensive of its owner and actively seeks out wealth and good fortune to bring to them. It is believed to be constantly on the lookout for opportunities to accumulate wealth.

keyMyths.title: The Emperor's Protection
keyMyths.description: Legend says the Pixiu assisted the Yellow Emperor in battle, leading to its association with imperial power and protection. Its ferocity and loyalty made it a favored symbol.

keyMyths.title: Attracting Wealth
keyMyths.description: The Pixiu's insatiable appetite for wealth and inability to excrete it symbolizes the accumulation and retention of fortune. This is the primary reason for its popularity in business and homes.
keyMyths.source: Chinese folklore and Feng Shui practices

sources.author: Lillian Too
sources.work: The Complete Illustrated Guide to Feng Shui
sources.description: Provides detailed information on the Pixiu's symbolism and use in Feng Shui.
sources.text: Provides detailed information on the Pixiu's symbolism and use in Feng Shui.

sources.author: Eva Wong
sources.work: Feng Shui: The Ancient Wisdom of Harmonious Living for Modern Times
sources.description: Offers insights into the cultural significance and practical applications of the Pixiu.
